concepts.finding:
Experimental evaluation shows that we outperform
state-of-the-art methods by large margins, in particular +26.6% on CI-
FAR10, +25.0% on CIFAR100-20 and +21.3% on STL10 in terms of clas-
siﬁcation accuracy.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:

large margins across multiple datasets.

The task of
unsupervised image classiﬁcation remains an important, and open chal-
lenge in computer vision.

concepts.finding:
Despite these eﬀorts, representation learning approaches are mainly used as the
ﬁrst pretraining stage of a two-stage pipeline.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
The proposed
method, named SCAN (Semantic Clustering by Adopting Nearest neighbors),
leverages the advantages of both representation and end-to-end learning ap-
proaches, but at the same time it addresses their shortcomings:

SCAN: Learning to Classify Images without Labels
3
– In a ﬁrst step, we le
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
In
contrast to representation learning approaches that require K-means clus-
tering after learning the feature representations, which is known to lead to
cluster degeneracy [4], we propose to mine the nearest neighbors of each
image based on feature similarity.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
First, we show
how mining nearest neighbors from a pretext task can be used as a prior for
semantic clustering.
concepts.implication: Applicable to user/item clustering for personalization

concepts.finding:
Also, we introduce additional constraints for selecting an
appropriate pretext task, capable of producing semantically meaningful feature
representations.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Second, we integrate the obtained prior into a novel loss function
to classify each image and its nearest neighbors together.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
Additionally, we show
how to mitigate the problem of noise inherent in the nearest neighbor selection
with a self-labeling approach.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
Section 3.2 experimentally shows that choosing
K ≥1 signiﬁcantly improves the results compared to only enforcing consistency
between samples and their augmentations, as in [24,21].
